sloka_794.meaning: they should encourage the ignorant in their duties
the confusion to neglect duties and cultivate knowledge
The learned, the teacher of jñāna-yoga

sloka_809.meaning: O Arjuna, noblest of the Bhāratas
and the destroyer of both jñān, knowledge of the self and non-self as delineated in the scriptures, and vijñān, realisation of such knowledge
which is the embodiment of sin

sloka_796.meaning: of the division of the modes of material nature (the three modes of goodness, passion, and ignorance), and action (the functional demigods, the senses, and the sense objects)
within the modes (in the form of the sense objects of sound, touch, form, taste, and smell)
is not attached to them, not absorbed in them

sloka_804.meaning: O descendant of the Vṛṣṇi dynasty

sloka_797.meaning: but should rather engage them in selfless action which frees them from absorption in the modes of nature
in the functions of the modes (the engagement of the senses with their objects)
should not agitate (by trying to induce them to accept knowledge of self and non-self)

sloka_771.meaning: by the yoga of selfless action
of those engaged in action in the world

sloka_784.meaning: thus established in the nature of cause and effect

sloka_780.meaning: by means of the five kinds of sacrifices, etc
without making any offerings (of that food)
The demigods (as limbs of My universal form)

sloka_800.meaning: completely deviated from the goal of human life

sloka_798.meaning: always conscious of the Spiritual Being

sloka_795.meaning: (by the senses, impelled) by the modes

sloka_783.meaning: are born of Akṣara, the Imperishable

sloka_782.meaning: From food (transformed into semen and blood)

sloka_786.meaning: depended on by him for any purpose
for that ātmārāma, one who delights in the self

sloka_775.meaning: without any desire for the results
the yoga of action as directed by the scriptures
is superior to such a hypocrite

sloka_787.meaning: being unattached to the fruits of your actions

sloka_777.meaning: all the living beings of this world
for the purpose of such sacrifice, for Him
as selfless duty offered to Lord Viṣṇu

sloka_811.meaning: the soul to be superior to the intelligence

sloka_810.meaning: the intelligence or faculty of decision

sloka_805.meaning: It is this desire to enjoy the mundane
It arises from the mode of passion, and from that desire, blind anger is born
and the worst enemy of the living being

sloka_769.meaning: in the terrible action of warfare?
do You urge me to engage

sloka_778.meaning: the progeny of the world, with sacrifices for Lord Viṣṇu

sloka_788.meaning: The learned, such as King Janaka and others
in the consideration of the welfare of the people

sloka_774.meaning: the senses of action the hands, legs, etc. (see 13.6)

sloka_799.meaning: from the bondage of karma, or action and reaction in the mundane plane
teaching of Mine, of selfless action

sloka_801.meaning: What will be the use of

sloka_773.meaning: by the modes or qualities born of material nature

sloka_772.meaning: to the state of spiritual consciousness beyond material activity

sloka_803.meaning: on one’s own path of duty (according to one’s appropiate position in the varṇāśram or God-centred socio-religious system)

sloka_781.meaning: from all sin (pañcha-sūnā, five types of sin due to five kinds of violence to other living beings)
who partake of the remnants of sacrifice to the demigods (universal administrators subordinate to the Lord)

sloka_779.meaning: and the demigods being thus pleased

sloka_770.meaning: by their ambiguous meanings, sometimes praising action and sometimes praising wisdom
